title: Camissoniopsis micrantha
intro: Camissoniopsis micrantha, also known as Western Evening Primrose, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae) that is native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Camissoniopsis micrantha contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is also used in the production of salves and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Camissoniopsis micrantha is classified in the family Polemoniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the jacobsenia or greasewood family. This family is part of the order Polemoniales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Polemoniales is a part of the clade Asterids, which includes several other orders of flowering plants. Within the family Polemoniaceae, Camissoniopsis micrantha is a genus of flowering plants native to the western United States. Camissoniopsis micrantha is specifically classified as a species within the Camissoniopsis genus.
uses: Camissoniopsis micrantha is a plant species, also known as the yellow owl's clover, and it has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people of California.
